title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at House of Blues with The Chats opening on March 20, 2026.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist showcased his energetic stage presence and engaged audiences with new and classic songs.
The Hives and The Chats delivered tight performances, blending rock and showmanship with acclaimed guitars and vocal styles.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Following a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh returned to running after treatment, inspired by a late friend.
This marathon marks McHugh's third Boston Marathon and fourth overall, running for charities she supports.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senator Ed Markey and colleagues warn Meta about facial recognition in smart glasses threatening privacy and public protest.
The senators demand answers by April 6 on how Meta plans to protect biometric data in proposed smart glasses.
Upcoming 'No Kings' protests in Massachusetts highlight the privacy concerns surrounding potential misuse of surveillance technology.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65M plan to replace firefighting gear due to PFAS contamination.
Firefighters and union leaders urged swift approval for new PFAS-free gear to reduce cancer risks among firefighters.
Some councilors want more time to assess the proposal, citing costs and durability of PFAS-free gear.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro plans to convert Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehabilitation center due to $57 million in needed repairs.
The proposal aims to reduce costs and address declining attendance, keeping the space open with free or reduced admission.
A transition team will form in July as debates continue, with the zoo operating as usual until September before limited access changes.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown newlyweds Abigail Brown and Colin Wong adapted their wedding plans due to a blizzard, opting for a microwedding.
FX's 'Love Story' series about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette reflects on 1990s communication styles.
Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters column discusses contemporary usage of the term 'partner' in relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback started running after his father's death from glioblastoma and his own battle with testicular cancer.
Despite setbacks, including a serious blood infection, Averback is committed to honoring his father and his survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots reportedly showed interest in trading for Eagles WR A.J. Brown, though an initial offer was declined.
Brown is working out with Patriots' S Kevin Byard, fueling speculation of a potential trade to New England.
Philadelphia may trade Brown after June 1 to save cap space, seeking a first-round draft pick or more.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ended his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Hagens is expected to sign with the Boston Bruins after Boston College's season-ending loss.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney is open to signing blue-chip prospects like Hagens post-trade deadline.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' features Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan, streaming on Netflix starting March 2026.
'Is This Thing On?', directed by Bradley Cooper, is a comedy-drama starring Will Arnett, available on Disney+.
The docuseries 'Born to Bowl', narrated by Liev Schreiber, is streamable on HBO Max.
